messages.content:
This script calculates the total amount of money paid by different individuals for different items like food, drinks, decoration, and paintings. The script has a float variable FullTotal to keep track of the total amount and also has separate variables totalV, totalS, totalR, totalP, and totalA for each individual.

The Start() method calls the PaidAmount() method for each individual and adds the returned value to the FullTotal variable. It also logs the total amount of money spent for all individuals.

The PaidAmount() method calculates the total amount paid by an individual for all items and logs the amount spent by that individual. It takes three parameters: total, which is the total amount spent by the individual, unit, which is the unit of the individual (like Vin, Sen, Rue, Pur, Ama), and name, which is the name of the individual.

It uses four for loops to iterate through all items (paintings, food, drinks, and decoration) and checks if the paidBy field in each item matches the unit of the individual. If it does, it adds the price of the item to the total amount spent by that individual.

The method sets the local variable s to the total amount spent by the individual and logs the name of the individual and the amount they spent.

Overall, this script is used to track the total amount spent by each individual for different items and keep track of the total amount spent by everyone.
how to add together the amonut spent by everyone

To add together the amount spent by everyone, you can add up all the individual totals stored in the FullTotal variable. This variable is updated in the Start() method after calling the PaidAmount() method for each individual.

To access the FullTotal variable from another script, you can make it a public static variable, like this:

```
public static float FullTotal = 0;
```

Then, you can access it in another script using the name of the script followed by a dot operator and the name of the variable, like this:

```
float totalAmount = SpendingTracker.FullTotal;
```

This will give you the total amount spent by everyone so far. You can update this variable continuously as more individuals spend money by adding their individual totals to it.
it is still 0 because totalV, totalS etc doesnt update
how do i update the individual total
fulltotal is 29062, divide that by 5 people

If the `FullTotal` value is 29062 and you want to split the cost between 5 people, you can divide the `FullTotal` by 5 to get the cost per person:

```
float costPerPerson = FullTotal / 5;
```

In this case, the `costPerPerson` is 5812.4 (rounded to one decimal place). This means that each person is responsible for paying around 5812.4 units of the currency used in this scenario. You can adjust the number of people accordingly if you want to split the cost differently.
how to write a code so the 5 people mentioned before will have paid 5812.4 if the total they have spent earlier are not equal

The `adjustment` variable is initialized to zero at the beginning of the `CalculateEqualShare()` method because we want to keep track of the total adjustment needed for all individuals. The adjustment that is needed for each individual is then added to this variable as we loop through the individual totals.

If an individual's total is greater than the average cost, it means they should pay less, so their total is adjusted to match the average cost, and the amount of adjustment needed for that individual is added to the `adjustment` variable.

If the `adjustment` is still zero after checking all individual totals, it means that everyone's total is already equal to or less than the average cost per person. In that case, we don't need to distribute any additional money among the individuals, so we skip the second part of the code that distributes the adjustment.
okay the adjustments are done, some people need to pay up while 2 only need to receive the money from the other 3, how do i write a code to see who pays who
